description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in V8's heap teardown process that can be triggered via sandbox memory corruption. The attacker uses Sandbox.MemoryView to write 0x1415 to sandbox offset 0x40000, setting the LARGE_PAGE flag (bit 10) in the MemoryChunk flags of a normal OLD_SPACE page. This causes the page to be tracked by LargeObjectSpace. During Heap::TearDown(), LargeObjectSpace::TearDown() (large-spaces.cc:73) frees the page's C++ metadata via delete cast to LargePageMetadata*, while the same page is simultaneously present in the page pool with its active_system_pages_ reset to null. When PagePool::ReleaseOnTearDown() subsequently calls DeleteMemoryChunk() on this pooled page, MutablePageMetadata::IsLargePage() reads the already-freed active_system_pages_ field, resulting in a heap-use-after-free. The root cause is the ambiguous IsLargePage() check (active_system_pages_.get() == nullptr), which returns true for both actual large pages and pooled pages, combined with the TearDown ordering that frees large-space metadata before releasing the page pool.

fixes.description: Upstream fix patch exported from commit 41614e86a3e9f3b94f7bb36d83cd9f0fb1c52a17 for fixed-image builds.
Upstream fix patch exported from commit 21fcc74333ed48097025343dcdb70227e04f933c for fixed-image builds.
